articles.title: Getting a Second Shot to Stop Meningitis
articles.summary: In Kent, a place in England, there was a sickness called meningitis B that made some people very sick and sadly two people died. To help stop this sickness, doctors gave a special shot called a vaccine to almost 12,000 people who might have been near the sickness. Now, these people will get a second shot starting next week to make sure they are protected. The shots are given at clinics in towns like Canterbury, Faversham, and Ashford. People can book their shot online. The sickness started at a nightclub and spread to university students and school pupils. The vaccine helps fight the bacteria that cause meningitis B. Getting two shots is important, with the second one given at least four weeks after the first. This is part of a bigger effort by the NHS to keep everyone safe.

articles.title: Astronauts Return Safely from Moon Mission
articles.summary: Four astronauts from NASA's Artemis II mission have returned safely to Earth after a trip around the Moon. They landed in the Pacific Ocean using a spacecraft called Orion, which traveled very fast and got very hot when coming back. The astronauts are named Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. They are now on a ship and will go home to see their families. This mission helps NASA plan to send people to live on the Moon in the future. Everyone was happy when they landed safely.

articles.title: Astronauts Return Home from Space Adventure
articles.summary: The Artemis II astronauts have come back to Earth after a big space trip. They landed in the ocean near California. These astronauts went farther into space than anyone before. They are Christina Koch, Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Jeremy Hansen. In space, their bodies change because there is no gravity. Their muscles and bones can get weaker. But astronauts are trained to handle this. They say being in space is amazing and they would go again. Now, doctors will check them to make sure they are healthy. They will then go to a space center in Houston. Many people have been to space, but this crew's trip was short compared to others.

articles.title: Vice President Goes to Pakistan to Talk About Ending a War
articles.summary: Vice President JD Vance is traveling to Pakistan to meet with officials from Iran. He wants to help stop a war that has been going on for about six weeks between the U.S., Israel, and Iran. The talks are difficult because there are big demands and not much trust. President Trump said he might not support more talks after this meeting. At the same time, in Lebanon, people are recovering from recent attacks by Israel that destroyed buildings and killed many, including over 300 people. Residents are sad and want to live in peace instead of war. This story shows efforts to make peace and the suffering caused by the conflict.

articles.title: Big Price Rise and Space Adventure News
articles.summary: This news article talks about two main stories. First, prices for things like gas are going up fast, which is called inflation. A report says prices rose 3.3% from last year, the biggest increase since 2024. This makes people worried about money. Second, astronauts are coming back to Earth after a trip to the moon. They are in a capsule that will splash down in the ocean near San Diego. NASA played songs to wake them up, and the astronauts are brave. The article also mentions a new arch plan in Washington, D.C., but focuses on prices and space. It helps us understand what's happening in the world today.

articles.title: Why Fewer Babies Are Being Born in the U.S.
articles.summary: In the United States, fewer babies are being born now than before. This is called a low birth rate. Since 2007, the number of babies born has dropped by 23%. This means there are not enough new children to replace people who grow old or pass away. If this continues, by 2055, the U.S. might have 8 million fewer people. But there is some good news: fewer teenagers are having babies, which experts say is positive. The article talks about why this is happening and what it could mean for the future, like having fewer workers or changes in schools. It is based on data from the CDC and includes an interview with a reporter from NPR.
